Overview Sources Spreadsheet

Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism · University of Oxford

AI Use Cases in
News Organisations

An overview of publicly documented AI use cases in news organisations worldwide, drawn from industry publications, research databases, and curated reports.

OpenAI. (2025). Pixel art illustration of a retro personal computer displaying a newspaper layout [Image generated by DALL-E]. ChatGPT. https://openai.com

—Loading
Insights

How AI adoption has evolved

Distribution of AI use cases across years of documented cases, broken down by functional task type, primary effect delivered, and stage in the news production process.

What AI is doing, by task type

Content generation has dominated since 2023, when large language models became widely accessible to newsrooms. Before then, the distribution of task types was broadly even.

Source: AI Use Cases in News Organisations, Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism, University of Oxford, 2026.

How we define these categories

Content generation: AI writes or produces original text, audio, or video content, including automated articles and data-driven reports.

Content transformation: AI reformats, summarises, or adapts existing content for a different platform, language, or audience.

Editing & optimisation: AI reviews or improves content for style, clarity, SEO, or engagement before publication.

Discovery & monitoring: AI tracks topics, sources, or events to surface relevant information for journalists or editors.

Data extraction & analysis: AI processes structured or unstructured data to extract facts, trends, or patterns for reporting.

Search & retrieval: AI enables search across large archives, databases, or documents to surface relevant material.

Transcription & translation: AI converts speech to text, or translates content between languages, to support production or access.

Verification & validation: AI checks facts, detects misinformation, or assesses the credibility of claims and sources.

Audience targeting & personalisation: AI tailors content, recommendations, or delivery to individual users based on behaviour or preferences.

Commercial optimisation: AI supports advertising, subscription, or revenue operations through targeting, pricing, or prediction.

Moderation & interaction: AI manages audience comments, detects harmful content, or powers conversational interfaces for readers.

Simon, F. M. (2025). Rationalisation of the news: How AI reshapes and retools the gatekeeping processes of news organisations in the United Kingdom, United States and Germany. New Media & Society. https://doi.org/10.1177/14614448251336423

What AI is delivering, by effect type

Efficiency gains and effectiveness at scale are reported in roughly equal measure, together accounting for 86% of all documented cases.

Source: AI Use Cases in News Organisations, Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism, University of Oxford, 2026.

How we define these categories

Efficiency: AI reduces time, cost, or effort for tasks that were previously done manually.

Effectiveness & scaling: AI enables tasks or volumes not previously possible, or dramatically expands what a team can produce.

Optimisation: AI improves quality, targeting, personalisation, or distribution without fundamentally changing the underlying task.

Simon, F. M. (2025). Rationalisation of the news: How AI reshapes and retools the gatekeeping processes of news organisations in the United Kingdom, United States and Germany. New Media & Society. https://doi.org/10.1177/14614448251336423

Where in the news is AI deployed, by gatekeeping stage

AI is most heavily deployed during content production and audience distribution. The selection and filtering stage — closest to core editorial judgment — sees the lowest adoption.

Source: AI Use Cases in News Organisations, Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism, University of Oxford, 2026.

How we define these categories

Access & observation: AI discovers, collects, or monitors information before it enters production. Includes trend detection, news monitoring, lead generation, and audience analytics for story ideas.

Selection & filtering: AI evaluates, verifies, or organises information during editorial vetting. Includes fact-checking, claim verification, transcription of source material, and document analysis.

Processing & editing: AI produces or refines news content. Includes automated writing, reformatting, summarisation, copy-editing, headline generation, and SEO optimisation.

Publishing & distribution: AI delivers content or manages audience interaction after publication. Includes personalisation, recommendation systems, comment moderation, and chatbot interfaces.

Domingo, D., Quandt, T., Heinonen, A., Paulussen, S., Singer, J. B., & Vujnovic, M. (2008). Participatory journalism practices in the media and beyond. Journalism Practice, 2(3), 326–342.    Shoemaker, P. J., & Vos, T. (2009). Gatekeeping theory. Routledge.
